groups.title: .agents · conceptual layer
groups.description: Defines workflow roles, responsibilities, and skills.

items.label: AGENT_MAPPING.md
items.whatItIs: Bridge between conceptual agents and runtime execution configuration.
items.whenToRead: After understanding AGENTS.md and before editing .codex.
items.whyItMatters: Prevents confusion between conceptual roles and runtime implementation.
commonMistakes: Changing runtime without checking the mapping

items.label: *.agent.md
items.whatItIs: Set of files defining expected behavior for planner, dev, reviewer, and QA roles.
items.whenToRead: When you want to understand who does what in the official flow.
items.whyItMatters: Provides a mental structure for the workflow and reduces responsibility mixing.
commonMistakes: Using one role to solve everything
Ignoring differences between qa-strategy, qa-functional, and qa-regression

items.label: skills/*/SKILL.md
items.whatItIs: Specialized protocols for planning, safe changes, reviewing, and testing.
items.whenToRead: Before non-trivial tasks or whenever work changes phase.
items.whyItMatters: Turns best practices into concrete repeatable steps.
commonMistakes: Treating a skill as passive documentation
Not loading the skill before editing files

groups.title: .codex · runtime layer
groups.description: Configures how agent execution is materialized at runtime.

items.label: builder.toml
items.whenToRead: After reviewing .agents if you want to understand the operational layer.
items.whyItMatters: Turns conceptual intent into executable behavior.
commonMistakes: Editing builder without validating conceptual impact
Assuming builder defines the entire strategy

items.label: planner.toml
items.whatItIs: Runtime config for the agent responsible for discovery and initial structuring.
items.whenToRead: When you want to see how the planner phase is operationalized.
items.whyItMatters: Keeps execution aligned with the requirement to start from understanding.
commonMistakes: Changing planner without updating conceptual expectations

items.label: qa.toml / reviewer.toml / security.toml
items.whatItIs: Runtime configs for validation, review, and security guardrails.
items.whenToRead: When examining how quality gates and guardrails are operationalized.
items.whyItMatters: Keeps the verification layer active in the workflow.
commonMistakes: Thinking QA and review are optional
Editing security without revisiting policy and risk

groups.title: Repository root · governance system
groups.description: Source-of-truth docs, changelog, overview, and policy files.

items.label: AGENTS.md
items.whatItIs: Main document defining the operating model and decision hierarchy.
items.whenToRead: First, whenever there is doubt about priority or process.
items.whyItMatters: It is the workflow source of truth.
commonMistakes: Changing workflow without reflecting it in AGENTS.md

items.label: AGENTS_CHANGELOG.md
items.whatItIs: Record of notable template and workflow changes.
items.whenToRead: When understanding system evolution or documenting important changes.
items.whyItMatters: Provides historical traceability and preserves decision context.
commonMistakes: Making structural changes without recording them
Using it like a README instead of a history log

items.label: AI_WORKFLOW.md / SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md / README.md / SECURITY.md
items.whatItIs: Documents explaining workflow, system overview, technical onboarding, and security policy.
items.whenToRead: During onboarding, before process changes, and when reviewing risks.
items.whyItMatters: Provides broad context, shared language, and safety boundaries.
commonMistakes: Reading only README and skipping SECURITY/OVERVIEW
Not aligning technical changes with global documentation
